author: @thesustainabilitysoonerchannel
text: Unsurprisingly,  poor Robert sliced his finger on the sharp edges of the sheet metal whirlybird during disassembly.  Great project!  But, be careful if you try it...  Robert, you're not the 1st.  Having experienced it, I feel your pain.🙄🤕😮‍💨. Thank God for Super Glue! 🤣

author: @gerryjamesedwards1227
text: I've used superglue to hold my fingertip together after cutting into it with a razor saw, there is a slight scar, but it's amazing stuff, and it stops anything getting in to the wound but allows the flesh to heal underneath.  It still seems to be not very widely known about, though.

replies.author: @girlsdrinkfeck
replies.text: yes but u should use medical  grade glue , bonding glue is toxic

author: @robertgaines-tulsa
text: I haven't seen those things around here in Oklahoma for around 20 years. I'd assume you can get them, but they have fallen out of favor for passive or solar ventilators. I think the modern thinking is that active ventilation pulls air into the attic from inside your house, so they've been recommending to only use passive ventilation. Our mid-60s roof only had these linear gabs covered with screen under the eves until like four years ago when the house was re-roofed and the roofers put in passive ventilators. I can't seem to find any visible openings in the attic, but it is dark up there, so maybe I don't know where to look. I saw a video where someone fed antenna leads through his ridge line vents. All you could see was a thin gap. It didn't even look like there was venting there, but the antenna leads fed through that crack.

author: @richbuilds_com
text: Would it work if you just mounted it on a black walled box (say 1m cubed) with a vent at the bottom for new air to be drawn in through?

author: @richbuilds_com
text: That is a sellable product mate. Put multiples on your roof!

author: @RandomAxeOfKindness
text: Mount this on the top of a tall black metal conduit somewhere in full sunlight, with vents at the bottom, and if the wind doesn't turn it, air going up the conduit will, at least on halfway sunny days.
